$comment: C23 _BitInt(N>64) MULTI-LIMB bit-precise integers (D-CSUBSET-BITINT-C2-WIDE), C2: N>64 as ceil(N/64) little-endian i64 limbs, alloca-backed like a struct, reached BY ADDRESS. Exercises + - & | ^ ~ << >> (constant AND variable count) == < >, int<->wide and wide->wide conversions, a BY-VALUE wide arg + wide return (InRegisters 128b two-GPR + by-reference 200b sret), a carry/borrow that CROSSES a limb boundary, and the TOP-LIMB MASK (a bit set past N reads back 0). RED-ON-DISABLE: the exit 42 is load-bearing on the multi-limb machinery — a NON-CARRYING impl loses the >>64 high limb, a NON-MASKING impl keeps the overflowed top bit, a LOW-LIMB-ONLY impl reads 0 for every high-limb probe; each changes the exit. Wide arithmetic is materialized by ADDRESS (never a bare SSA aggregate), so the release arm's mem2reg/const-fold preserve the And-masks + carry loops and still exit 42.
